Erythrococca zeylanica is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erythrococca zeylanica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Erythrococca zeylanica is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritation.
